skills.description: ngspice simulation tutorial and template skill. Built-in PTM 180/45/22nm models included.
skills.author: Arcadia-1

skills.name: gmoverid
skills.description: gm/ID transistor characterization and design methodology, based on ngspice + Python. Two independent workflows: (1) Characterization — generates three standard curve sets for any MOSFET model: gate capacitance (Cgg/Cgs/Cgd/Cgb vs Vgs), gm/ID four-quadrant characteristics (gm/Id vs Vov, Id/W vs gm/Id, fT vs gm/Id, gm·ro vs gm/Id), and IV characteristics (linear/log Id vs Vov, output curves). Supports 180 nm single-node and 45/22 nm HP multi-node flows with built-in PTM model files (180/45/22 nm) — no extra downloads required. (2) Design — the GmIdTable class builds a lookup table from simulation data (cached to logs/cache/) and provides lookup(), size(), size_from_ft(), size_from_gmro() APIs for NMOS/PMOS transistor sizing using the gm/ID methodology. Only depends on the ngspice skill. Use this skill when setting up or extending a gm/ID characterization project, generating characteristic curves, interpreting design curves, or sizing transistors by the gm/ID method.
skills.author: Arcadia-1

skills.name: transistor-models
skills.description: Complete PTM (Predictive Technology Model) MOSFET model library from mec.umn.edu/ptm, covering all nodes: bulk conventional 180/130/90/65nm, bulk HP/LP 45/32/22nm (BSIM4), and PTM-MG multi-gate FinFET 7/10/14/16/20nm (BSIM-CMG, HP + LSTP). No manual downloads required after installing this skill. Independent of the gmoverid skill — can be used directly in any ngspice/HSPICE project.

skills.name: review-ownership-manifest

skills.author: cwilliams5

skills.name: review-race-conditions-open
skills.description: Open Audit for race conditions in timers, hotkeys, callbacks, and shared state
skills.author: cwilliams5

skills.name: flight-recorder
skills.description: Analyze flight recorder dumps to diagnose bugs from event traces
skills.author: cwilliams5

skills.name: review-blocking
skills.description: Discover functions that block the main thread, then micro-audit each for internal optimization opportunities
skills.author: cwilliams5

skills.name: review-file-size
skills.description: Find files approaching context limits and evaluate whether coupling-aware splits are worthwhile
skills.author: cwilliams5

skills.name: merge-all

skills.author: cwilliams5

skills.author: cwilliams5

skills.name: review-comments
skills.description: Audit comments for staleness — stale identifier references, outdated behavioral claims, and unverifiable flags

skills.name: followup-tests
skills.description: Evaluate whether changes suggest new tests, modified tests, or new static analysis checks

skills.name: nd-core
skills.description: Use when communicating with neurodivergent users — adapts response structure, clarity, and pacing for ADHD, autism, and other neurotypes. Recommended base for all nd-* skills.
skills.author: Ktiseos-Nyx

skills.name: sounding-board
skills.description: Use when a user needs to vent, talk something out, or just not feel like they're shouting into a void. Active listening with gentle reflection — not therapy, not silence, just a friend who pays attention.
skills.author: Ktiseos-Nyx

skills.name: nd-focus-support
skills.description: Use when user has ADHD or attention differences — tracks current task, catches conversation drift, and nudges back on topic without judgment or guilt.
skills.author: Ktiseos-Nyx

skills.name: nd-plural-support
skills.description: Use when user is part of a plural system — provides warm session check-in for who is fronting and maintains a simple JSON session log. No clinical language, no assumptions about system structure.
skills.author: Ktiseos-Nyx

skills.name: lora-trainer-guide
skills.description: Use when helping users configure LoRA training — select presets, understand parameters, and pick the right settings for their model type. Covers SD1.5, SDXL, SD3, and Flux1 with real-world presets.
skills.author: Ktiseos-Nyx

skills.name: nd-frustration-support
skills.description: Use when user shows signs of overwhelm or frustration — repeated failed attempts, tone shifts, sudden brevity, topic abandonment. Guides gentle check-ins without being patronizing or clinical.
skills.author: Ktiseos-Nyx
